pattern: Pre-sorting indices by (value, index) lets the whole marking simulation run in one linear pass instead of repeatedly rescanning for the next smallest survivor.
hints: The order elements get marked in only depends on value and, for ties, index -- so decide that whole order up front.
Sort the indices by (value, index) once, then sweep through that order, skipping anything already marked by a previous pick.
Each time you land on an unmarked index, add its value to the score and mark it plus its immediate left and right neighbors before moving on.

edge_inputs.description: marking a middle pick also removes both neighbors from future picks
ties on value broken by index
two elements, picking one always marks both
all identical values, order decided purely by index
smallest value sits at an end, so it has only one neighbor to mark
strictly increasing, greedy must still restart from the true minimum each round
strictly decreasing, mirrors the increasing case

stress.description: large random array near the length ceiling with repeated values
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 1000000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
